mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, compressing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, lifting equity cash flow projections and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations across a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which reduce input cost inflation and support equity multiples, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO prints consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index breaching 240.0 triggers inflation risk premium repricing, forcing equity risk premiums higher and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL crosses 240.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands aggregate corporate revenue bases, directly supporting earnings per share growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rate combined with stagnant payroll growth confirms labor market stagnation, signaling weak consumer spending power and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in walk-forward testing with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1200 thousand units during periods of positive real GDP growth confirms robust fixed investment and construction activity, which historically precedes equity market rallies and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.
